summary: Completed comprehensive testing of InviteAI Canva-style template system redesign. Template gallery, filters, search, authentication, and customization flow all work perfectly. Backend generation API fixed (was 500 error). Critical frontend issue found on Results page preventing display of generated designs.

fixed_bugs.fix_applied: Removed _id field from response to prevent MongoDB ObjectId serialization issues

critical_bugs.issue: React runtime error: 'object is not iterable (cannot read property Symbol(Symbol.iterator))' in Results component
critical_bugs.impact: Blocks users from seeing generated wedding card designs

working_features: Template Gallery loads correctly with 17 templates
Category filters (All, Traditional, Modern, Floral, Regional) work perfectly
Religion filters (All, Hindu, Muslim, Sikh, Christian) work perfectly
Template cards show name, tagline, features properly
Popular badges display on correct templates
Authentication flow redirects to Emergent OAuth when not logged in
Customization page loads with proper authentication
Live preview updates correctly with form input
All form fields functional: bride name, groom name, date, venue
Add English checkbox appears for non-English languages
Video upgrade options with 3 styles work
Generate button successfully creates wedding and redirects

passed_tests: Backend API health check (9/9 tests passed - 100% success rate)
Add English checkbox for non-English languages
Video upgrade checkbox with 3 style options

success_percentage.backend: 100% - All APIs working, generation issue fixed
success_percentage.frontend_ui: 95% - All UI components work except Results page display
success_percentage.integration: 90% - Full flow works until final results display

critical: Fix Results.jsx component React iteration error on line ~72
Ensure designsData is properly handled as array in Results component
Add error boundary to Results component to handle data loading errors gracefully
action_item_for_main_agent.description: The main user flow works perfectly until the final step where users should see their generated designs. The Results page crashes with a React iterator error preventing design display. This is likely a data handling issue in the frontend component.
